## Frame 3 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium, chest-up to mid-forearm, subject fills about 60% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a slow continuing push with a gentle 5° arc following the start of her pivot (one flowing move), otherwise stable.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0.

CHOREOGRAPHY & FLUID MOTION: her ribbon arm extends up on a light breath as the body begins a buoyant chaine pivot and the opposite arm counter-balances soft — a continuous rhythmic-gymnastics turn that grows directly out of the previous figure-eight, soft joints, buoyant and spiralling, the dance and the blossoming ribbon moving as one. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) the ribbon extends up and out; (b) the tail dissolves into the first petals; (c) the body initiates the chaine pivot; (d) the skirt sweeps wide.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–3.0s — Aira sweeps the ribbon up and out, the trailing tail beginning to dissolve into the first swirl of petals as she initiates a light chaine pivot.
- 3.0–6.0s — the petals lift and spiral as her eyes brighten with fresh delight and the skirt sweeps wide. One clean continuous turn, real petal physics.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: life never stops — breath moving the chest, the loose waves and wisps lifting with the pivot, micro weight-shifts floating through the supporting foot, the ribbon arm carrying a living follow-through, living eyes and a slow blink; no freeze, no stiffness, the pivot buoyant and fluid, never robotic.

LIGHTING: warm golden key with soft fill; a golden rim igniting the ribbon-edge and petals live as they lift; the sky held airy; the low sun flaring with the motion.

ENVIRONMENT: rooftop garden, the first petals lifting and spiralling in the golden air, the skirt sweeping wide.

AUDIO: diegetic only. Ambient — soft warm breeze and birdsong. SFX — a soft satin-ribbon whoosh at 0.8s, a gentle rising petal-flutter from 1.6s as the ribbon blossoms, a soft footwork-brush on stone at 3.2s as she pivots. Reaction — a soft delighted breath at 3.0s. The master track lifts — the harp and strings joined by a light woodwind and a gentle building beat under the flutter (present, never full). VO — none this frame.

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:06–0:09 of 18s total).

LIP-SYNC: none.

MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) the mist draws back to its Frame 1 collarbone cradle; (b) the ribbon wand lifts back into the opening curl; (c) the spine lifts tall into the releve; (d) the front foot rises light onto the ball — a connective wave returns the body, never a hard reset, leaving it buoyant and breathing.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) the breeze returning to its opening drift through the blossoms; (2) the petal-trim releasing back into the reforming ribbon; (3) a petal drifting at the shoulder as at the open; (4) a face-framing wisp stirring at the temple matched to Frame 1; (5) chest rising on a light inhale matched to the open; (6) golden speculars sliding to their Frame 1 position on the frosted bottle; (7) the ribbon-tail carrying its opening curl; (8) a slow eyelash settle completing, matched to the open.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the frosted mist bottle and cradling fingers at roughly 20% frame depth, tack sharp, matched to Frame 1. MIDGROUND — Aira centered in the light releve, three-quarter torso. BACKGROUND — the blooming cherry tree and soft skyline melting to warm golden haze with one softer gradient behind her head, drifting petals, matched to Frame 1.

LIGHTING: KEY — a warm low golden-hour key feathered from above-side, matched to Frame 1. FILL — a soft warm bounce below. RIM — a golden rim on the hair and shoulder. PRACTICAL — the low sun flaring soft through blossoms. SEPARATION — the sky held airy and bright. BOUNCE — a warm gold kick off the bottle glass.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium close, chest-up, 13% headroom, the bottle in the lower third, bright airy negative space camera-left — matched exactly to Frame 1 for the seamless loop.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: The loop-close frame matches Frame 1 exactly — chest-up medium-close, about 13% headroom, the bottle in the lower third, bright airy negative space camera-left. Aira returns to the opening light buoyant stance: centred, the front foot rising into a soft relevé, weight poised forward, near-frontal. Camera distance and composition match the start so the final instant rhymes back into the first for a seamless loop, the presenting stance lifting back into the opening relevé — the circle closed. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

## Frame 6 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium close, chest-up, subject fills about 55% of the vertical frame, matched to Frame 1.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a slow 8% pull-back exactly reversing Frame 1's push so the end framing lands on the opening framing (one flowing move) for a seamless loop, stable.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0.

CHOREOGRAPHY & FLUID MOTION: the presenting stance lifts smoothly back into the opening releve and ribbon curl — a continuous return flowing out of Frame 5's line, the mist drawing back to its Frame 1 cradle and the ribbon reforming on the wand, the body breathing and buoyant throughout so the loop point is alive rather than a frozen reset. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) the mist draws back to the collarbone cradle; (b) the ribbon wand lifts into the opening curl; (c) the spine lifts tall into the releve; (d) the front foot rises light onto the ball.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–4.0s — Aira settles the line as the woven petal-trim releases back into the long satin ribbon and the last petals drift back to the breeze, drawing the mist back toward the opening cradle.
- 4.0–6.0s — the camera pulls gently back as her stance and fresh expression resolve exactly onto Frame 1, the light releve and ribbon curl matched so the cut loops invisibly. One clean continuous phrase, real physics throughout.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: continuous life into the loop — breath lifting the chest, the loose waves bouncing and settling, a face-framing wisp stirring, petals drifting and resettling, micro weight-shifts lifting through the releve foot, living eye saccades and a slow blink matched to Frame 1; no freeze, no robotic reset, the loop point buoyant and breathing.

LIGHTING: the warm golden key resolving to the exact Frame 1 state as the petal-trim releases; a golden rim on the hair and shoulder; the sky held airy and bright; sun flaring soft through blossoms.

ENVIRONMENT: rooftop garden restored to its opening golden hush, the ribbon reformed, petals drifting on the breeze.

AUDIO: diegetic only. Ambient — soft warm breeze and gentle birdsong settling to match the open. SFX — a soft petal-rustle at 1.2s, a gentle satin-ribbon whoosh as the ribbon reforms at 2.8s, a soft fabric-settle as the stance lifts at 4.0s, easing to the exact opening ambience for a seamless audio loop. Reaction — a light breath at 4.8s. The master track resolves its phrase and dips back to the lone plucked harp of the open so the end flows back into the beginning (present, never full).

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:15–0:18 of 18s total).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps acquisition, 180° shutter, natural motion blur on the settle and pull-back; playback real-time, the final frame matched to Frame 1 for an invisible loop.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips resolve to the softly-parted fresh Frame 1 state.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, double-exposure unless intended, audio desync, wardrobe drift, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (ribbon/petal/breeze/hair/dress/light), petals touching the face or skin, loop-mismatch with Frame 1, mannequin posture, robotic snapping motion, stiff joints, frozen background, identity flicker.

MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Concept 14 — full reel, lands across the cut, anchored at the Frame 4 petal-storm turn and the Frame 5 line): a fresh airy orchestral-pop score for an 18-second vertical beauty reel. STYLE/GENRE — bright uplifting spring orchestral-pop, light and buoyant. INSTRUMENTATION — a plucked harp, soft layered strings, a gentle piano, light woodwind (flute), a soft hand-percussion beat and a warm sustained pad. TEMPO & KEY — about 104 BPM, a gentle 4/4 in a bright, warm major. ARRANGEMENT ACROSS THE CUT — 0:00–0:03 a lone plucked harp and a faint pad kindling under the birdsong (Frame 1); 0:03–0:06 soft strings and a gentle piano and beat building as she mists and spins the ribbon (Frame 2); 0:06–0:09 a light flute and a building beat lift as the ribbon blossoms into the first petals (Frame 3 transform-start); 0:09–0:12 harp, soaring strings, woodwind and a gentle uplifting beat crest with the full petal-storm chaine turn (Frame 4 transform-peak, present and bright, still never full); 0:12–0:15 the mix ducks clean under the spoken line “bloom into spring.” (Frame 5); 0:15–0:18 the phrase resolves and dips back to the lone plucked harp of the open so the end loops seamlessly into the beginning (Frame 6, AX.5). MIX RULE — the score stays underneath the diegetic breeze, birdsong, satin-ribbon whoosh and petal-flutter SFX and ducks clean for the vocal — airy and present, never a full driving mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

# CONCEPT 15 — CRYSTAL CAVE TAI-CHI

Title: Crystal Cave Tai-Chi (Pillar 9 — Mystic Grounding)

Format: Mystic Grounding Beauty Hero / Tai-Chi Geode-Cave Editorial — cool crystalline lane (luminous subterranean mineral glow; the deliberate tonal opposite of Concept 14's bright pastel daylight).

Hero Product: "Amethyst Dew" pressed crystal-serum — a faceted amethyst-glass jar cut like a raw geode, brushed-silver collar, quartz-point cap, holding a translucent lilac gel-serum that catches cave light into tiny violet refractions; a single bright dew-bead rests on the quartz cap.

Setting: a vast subterranean crystal cavern at the still hour — cathedral walls of raw amethyst and clear-quartz formations, towering geode clusters, a glass-smooth mineral reflecting pool, slow drifting motes, a single shaft of pale daylight falling from a fissure far overhead, cool teal-violet ambient glow rising from the crystals themselves.

Wardrobe: a flowing tai-chi-inspired ceremonial set in pale crystalline lilac: a long wide-sleeved wrap top over softly draped wide-leg trousers in weighty matte silk-crepe, high modest neckline, a soft sash at the waist; long unbroken lines that trail and float on every weight-shift; fully modest.

Hair: a sleek low softly coiled chignon with a clean centre part and a faint mineral sheen, two slim quartz hairpins catching light, a few soft face-framing strands that drift on the turns.

Makeup: dewy crystalline glow: a sheer lilac-pearl wash on the lid, glass-skin luminosity with cool highlight on cheekbone and brow-bone, soft mauve-rose blush, groomed fluffy brows, a hydrated mauve-pink satin lip (real pores and texture preserved throughout).

Choreography: continuous slow tai-chi flow — grounded opening stance, cloud hands, part-the-wild-horse's-mane, white-crane-spreads-its-wings, single-whip, brush-knee, and a slow grounding sink; every transfer of weight is unbroken, soft-jointed and rooted, breath-paced and meditative, never posed or frozen.

Movement / Transform: geode-crystal growth bloom (AX.5) — at the transform beat, fine crystalline facets nucleate along the hem and sleeve and spread outward in a slow blooming lattice that races up the cave walls and across the geode clusters in answer to her gesture, light splintering into violet refractions, then settling as a delicate faceted crystalline trim woven along sleeve and hem. Growth touches garment, mineral, air and light only — never skin or face.

Concept Line: NONE — silent reel.

Duration: 21s | 7 frames (3s each).

Mood: MYSTIC GROUNDING — serene, weighty, luminous, otherworldly-calm.

Composition: 9:16 vertical locked; full-body and waist-up alternation to read both the long-line flow and the dewy product close; deep cavern negative space; reflecting-pool mirror used for symmetry.

Grade: cool crystalline vibrance — amethyst-violet and quartz-white with a cool teal mineral undertone, luminous lifted shadows that never crush to mud, clean specular sparkle on facets, bright but cool; kept vivid and contemporary, never desaturated.

Spoken line: NONE — silent reel (closed-lip serene composure throughout; zero lip-sync on any frame).

Audio note: fully diegetic-led — deep cave-tone drone, faint water-drip, soft crystalline chime on the bloom, breath-soft sub-pulse; no vocal.

Identity safety: AIRA — (PLACEHOLDER — paste Aira character reference sheet / face-lock image here). Lock face, bone structure, hair color and base texture to the reference; zero drift; adult, non-sexualized. Tools: Nano Banana Pro (stills) + VEO 3.1 (video), 9:16 vertical, Aira age 25; across all seven frames her movement is slow, grounded and continuous.

COLOR GRADE: cool crystalline vibrance — amethyst-violet and quartz-white with a cool teal undertone, luminous lifted shadows, clean facet sparkle, bright but cool, vivid and contemporary, never desaturated.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: the settled crystal trim reads as true faceted quartz with crisp refraction and clean edges; the faceted jar shows violet refractions and a clean label window; the silk-crepe holds a real matte weave; real skin peach-fuzz at the jaw catching the cool rim.

NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, inverted, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes (unintentional), garbled text, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, low-res patches, plastic skin, over-smoothed skin, oversharpened, AI-generated artifacts, watermarks, signature, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts at ankle/knee/wrist/elbow/neck, amputated fingertips, cross-eyed, lazy eye, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin-stiff posture, dead eyes, statue-frozen energy, garbled label text, crushed muddy blacks, garish oversaturation, flat dull light.

## Frame 5 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. # CONCEPT 16 — PEACOCK BHARATANATYAM PALACE

Title: Peacock Bharatanatyam Palace (Pillar 10 — Regal Heritage)

Format: Regal Indian-Classical Beauty Hero / Bharatanatyam Durbar Editorial — warm jewel-tone lane (opulent golden lamplight and peacock jewel-colour; the deliberate warm regal counter to Concept 15's cool crystal cave).

Hero Product: "Mayura" jewel-teal kohl-and-liner — an ornate peacock-embossed gold vial with a fine sculpted-feather cap and a precise brush wand, holding an intense iridescent peacock-teal-to-green liner that shifts colour in the lamplight; accent hero a single bright liner-bead catching the brush tip.

Setting: an opulent palace durbar hall at the lamp-lit hour — carved honey-sandstone columns, jewel-inlaid scalloped arches with peacock motifs, rows of warm flickering diya oil-lamps, a polished marble floor with peacock-blue and gold pietra-dura inlay, rich sapphire-and-gold drapes, deep warm shadow beyond the lamps (rich warm regal lane, NOT cool or plain).

Wardrobe: a regal Bharatanatyam costume in jewel-teal and royal-gold silk: a modest high-neck full-sleeve fitted blouse and a richly bordered pleated dance-skirt with the classic stitched fan-pleat, gold zari work, a modest layered drape across the chest, temple jewellery (gold waist-belt, layered haar necklaces, jhumka earrings, maang-tikka), fully modest, the silk drinking the warm lamplight into deep jewel folds.

Hair: a traditional sleek centre-parted low bun wound with a fresh white jasmine gajra and dressed with gold temple hair-ornaments (a jada down the braid, sun-and-moon pieces), a high silk gloss, a few soft face-framing strands, color locked to reference.

Makeup: vivid regal classical: a bold peacock-teal-and-gold graphic eye with crisp black kohl-rimmed liner winged long, a sharp gold highlight on the high points, soft-set strong brows, a deep rose-red satin lip, a small gold-and-red bindi, fresh dewy real skin with visible pores and fine vellus texture.

Choreography: Bharatanatyam — a grounded araimandi half-sit, precise expressive hasta mudras (alapadma, pataka, mayura), crisp adavu footwork with rhythmic heel-and-toe, a graceful spotting chakkar spin, elegant tribhanga curves and expressive abhinaya of the eyes and brows; movement is sharp-yet-fluid, rooted and regal, the lines clean and the wrists always alive, never a stiff held pose.

Movement / Transform: mayura mudra → feather-eye plume fan-out (the one impossible move) — on the peacock mudra her temple-jewellery and skirt-border bloom outward into a wide fan of iridescent peacock-feather plumes (the shimmering blue-green eye-of-the-feather) that spread behind her like a peacock's tail, then settle as jewelled feather-embellishment along the costume. Plumes grow from jewellery, border and air only — never from skin or face.

Concept Line: "regal by nature."

Duration: 21s | 7 frames (3s each).

Mood: REGAL VIVID — opulent, proud, jewel-bright, graceful.

Composition: 9:16 vertical locked; full-body araimandi and waist-up abhinaya alternation to read both the clean classical lines and the kohl-rimmed eyes; durbar-hall depth with warm lamp-bokeh; symmetrical regal framing.

Grade: warm jewel-tone vibrance — peacock teal-and-green with royal gold and deep sapphire, warm diya lamplight, rich uncrushed blacks, full jewel mid-tone, bright opulent vibrance, no harsh clipping (rich warm regal lane).

Spoken line: lands Frame 5 — "regal by nature." (English, natural Indian accent, proud and warm.)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic (soft diya-flame flicker, anklet ghungroo bells on the footwork, silk-and-jewellery rustle, a soft feather-shimmer on the bloom) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a regal Carnatic-fusion bed with mridangam, veena, soft nadaswaram and tanpura drone, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA — (PLACEHOLDER — paste Aira character reference sheet / face-lock image here). Lock face, bone structure, hair color and base texture to the reference; zero drift; adult, non-sexualized. Tools: Nano Banana Pro (stills) + VEO 3.1 (video), 9:16 vertical, Aira age 25; the costume is fully modest and the mood is regal and proud, never sexualized; across all seven frames her movement is sharp, rooted and graceful.

## Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

WARDROBE: the regal jewel-teal-and-royal-gold Bharatanatyam costume, the modest high-neck full-sleeve fitted blouse and richly bordered fan-pleat skirt catching the warm diya lamplight into deep jewel folds, gold zari border glinting, temple jewellery (waist-belt, layered haar, jhumkas, maang-tikka) catching warm points, fully modest, pristine, zero lint or snags.

HAIR: the sleek centre-parted low bun wound with a fresh white jasmine gajra and dressed with gold temple hair-ornaments (jada, sun-and-moon pieces), a high silk gloss, a few soft face-framing strands reading as real individual hairs, color locked to reference.

MAKEUP: vivid regal classical — a bold peacock-teal-and-gold graphic eye with crisp black kohl-rimmed liner winged long, a sharp gold highlight on cheekbone, brow-bone and nose-tip, soft-set strong brows, a deep rose-red satin lip, a small gold-and-red bindi, dewy real skin with visible pores and fine vellus texture.

HANDS & NAILS: right hand cradling the ornate peacock-embossed gold vial of Mayura at collarbone height, fingertips light on the gold with natural nail-bed color, no white-knuckle pressure; left hand raised in a crisp alapadma mudra (lotus hand), fingers fanned and alive; short almond nails in a deep glossy rose-red, henna-tipped, cuticles neat.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: chin lifted a regal 2°, a proud composed quarter-smile pulling one corner a hair tighter, lips closed in a serene set, eyes bright and direct to lens with crisp warm twin catchlights and a hint of expressive abhinaya at the brow, the faintest regal poise at the outer eye.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: grounded in a half-araimandi — knees softly turned out and bent, torso lifted tall and rotated about 5°, weight even and low between both rooted feet with a 2° hip settle, front shoulder soft, spine long and regal, crown lifted; mid-breath and alive (living stillness), clearly caught mid-phrase rather than held.

CHOREOGRAPHY FLOW: the opening beat of a Bharatanatyam phrase — her left hand is mid-bloom into alapadma, the gesture travelling shoulder → elbow → turning wrist → fanning fingertips, the spine lifted tall from a just-settled araimandi sink. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) the mudra hand blooms open from the wrist; (b) the spine lifts long out of the grounded sink; (c) the cradling arm holds the vial in a soft rounded line; (d) the rooted weight stays low and even; the frame catches the breath of suspension between two crisp classical movements, joints soft, lines clean.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) a single face-framing strand drifting in the warm draught; (2) the jewel-teal liner shifting its colour a hair inside the tilted gold vial; (3) a diya flame flickering off-frame throwing a living warm glow; (4) the fan-pleat skirt settling with a micro-shift of shadow; (5) chest rising on a regal shallow inhale; (6) warm speculars sliding along the embossed gold vial and feather cap; (7) her alapadma fingertips carrying the last millimetre of the bloom; (8) a slow eyelash settle completing a blink, jhumkas swaying a hair.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the gold vial and her cradling fingers at roughly 20% frame depth, tack sharp, label forward. MIDGROUND — Aira centred in araimandi, three-quarter torso, mudra hand raised. BACKGROUND — carved sandstone columns and a jewel-inlaid arch softly defocused with one warmer gradient behind her head, rows of diyas falling to warm shadow, no clutter.

LIGHTING: KEY — a warm soft beauty key feathered from above-front producing rich regal modelling. FILL — a soft warm bounce below lifting the under-chin just clean (gentle clamshell). RIM — a warm gold rim skimming the bun and shoulder to separate from the dark hall. PRACTICAL — living diya-glow pooling warm off-frame and glinting on the marble inlay. SEPARATION — the hall held deep so she reads about 2 stops up. BOUNCE — a warm gold kick adding a glossy catch to the vial.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium close, chest-up, 12% headroom, the vial entering the lower third, warm negative space camera-left to receive the feather-plume that arrives later.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A chest-up medium-close beauty frame, about 12% headroom, the vial in the lower third, warm negative space camera-left for the feather-plume. ## Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium close, chest-up, subject fills about 55% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a single slow 8% push-in across the clip, otherwise locked-off and stable — one move only, no whip.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0, smooth warm background fall-off.

CHOREOGRAPHY & FLUID MOTION: her left hand blooms slowly into an alapadma mudra — the gesture travelling shoulder → elbow → turning wrist → fanning fingertips in one unbroken line — while the spine lifts tall out of a grounded araimandi; rooted and controlled, the carriage breathing, with a clear flow-in and flow-out so no pose is ever held flat. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: the mudra hand blooms from the wrist, the spine lifts long, the cradling arm holds its rounded line, the rooted weight stays low and even.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–3.0s — Aira tilts the gold vial a few degrees into the warm key, speculars sliding along the embossed feather as her mudra hand completes its bloom.
- 3.0–6.0s — she lifts a proud regal gaze to lens on a slow inhale as the composed quarter-smile blooms and a flicker of brow-abhinaya plays. One clean continuous phrase, real physics throughout.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: never frozen — constant low-amplitude life: breath moving the chest and the silk folds, the gajra and jhumkas carrying a faint settle, a face-framing strand drifting, micro weight-shifts rolling through the rooted araimandi, the mudra wrist still turning a hair, living eyes with expressive saccades and a natural slow blink; absolutely no mannequin hold, no statue freeze, no robotic snap.

LIGHTING: warm regal key with soft warm fill; live gold speculars travelling across the vial and skin as it tips; the hall held deep; warm rim steady on the bun and shoulder; diya-flicker living off-frame.

ENVIRONMENT: opulent palace durbar hall, carved sandstone columns, jewel-inlaid arches, rows of diyas, peacock-inlay marble floor, drifting warm air.

AUDIO: diegetic only, no in-clip music. Ambient bed — a low warm hall tone and a faint diya-flame flicker throughout. SFX — a soft gold-vial tap as her fingers settle at 0.6s, a faint silk-and-jewellery rustle at 3.2s, a delicate jhumka-chime accent at 4.8s timed to the gaze-lift. Reaction — a soft proud breath at 3.0s. The master track sits low — a lone veena figure and a soft tanpura drone just kindling under the hall tone (present, never full). VO — none this frame.

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:00–0:03 of 21s total).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps acquisition, 180° shutter, natural motion blur on the mudra and vial tilt; playback 24fps real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips closed in a serene regal set.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, double-exposure unless intended, audio desync, wardrobe drift, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (feather/silk/jewellery/hair/light), mannequin posture, robotic snapping motion, stiff joints, frozen background, identity flicker.

ENVIRONMENT: palace durbar hall, the first peacock-plumes unfurling from her jewellery, the fan-pleat beginning to flare.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-wide, head-to-thigh, subject fills about 65% of the vertical frame.

## Frame 7 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. # CONCEPT 17 — BIOLUMINESCENT LIQUID-DANCE

Title: Bioluminescent Liquid-Dance (Pillar 11 — Luminous Wonder).

Format: Bioluminescent Glow-Liquid Beauty Hero / Blacklight Liquid-Light Editorial (cool neon-glow lane — a deliberate trippy, weightless counter to Concept 16's warm regal durbar; pure inky void lit only by UV-reactive bioluminescence).

Hero Product: "Lumen" glow-reactive liquid luminizer drops — a sleek frosted-glass dropper bottle whose contents glow living UV-cyan, the liquid shifting bioluminescent aqua-to-magenta as it catches the blacklight, a single luminous bead trembling at the dropper tip. Modern, weightless, magical.

Setting: a pitch-black infinity studio under UV/blacklight — no walls, no horizon, pure dark void; floating motes of luminescent plankton-like glow-dust drift in the air, shallow pools of reactive glow-liquid mirror the cyan-magenta light across a black glossy floor, suspended droplets hang and catch the UV, depth implied only by glow falloff.

Wardrobe: a sleek modest liquid-sheen catsuit-gown in deep navy-black, fully modest (high mock-neck, long sleeves, full-length, opaque), with fine bioluminescent piping that glows cyan-to-magenta along the seams and a softly luminous hem; describe styling and glow, never body shape.

Hair: a sleek wet-look high ponytail, slicked glassy crown, faint glow-gel threading the lengths so the tail reads as a ribbon of cyan light when it whips; high gloss, color locked to reference.

Makeup: luminous UV-reactive glow — a cyan-to-magenta gradient graphic eye that glows under blacklight, dewy glass skin with a bioluminescent highlight on the cheekbones and brow-bone, a glossy iridescent oil-slick lip, a scatter of tiny glow-dots tracing the temples, soft-set strong brows, dewy real skin; short almond glass-glossy iridescent nails with luminous tips.

Choreography: liquid-dance — fluid body-waves, arm-waves and finger-tutting geometry, slow liquid isolations, waacking-arc flourishes and hand-flow, the body moving like glow-liquid in zero gravity, every joint melting into the next; hypnotic, weightless, never stiff, wrists and fingers always alive.

Movement / Transform: glow-paint splatter bloom (AX.5) — on a sharp tut-freeze, droplets of glow-liquid burst from her fingertips and seam-piping and bloom outward into a suspended constellation of bioluminescent splatter that hangs in the air like liquid galaxies, then settles as luminous body-art tracing the piping; the splatter grows from piping, droplets and air only, never from skin or face.

Concept Line: "glow from within."

Duration: 18s | 6 frames (3s each).

Mood: TRIPPY LUMINOUS.

Composition: 9:16 vertical; the glowing subject isolated in pure black void, glow falloff carving the depth, generous dark negative space.

Grade: cool bioluminescent vibrance — electric cyan, glow magenta, deep violet and UV-blue over pure inky uncrushed black, luminous neon mid-tones, bright glow vibrance, no harsh clipping.

Spoken line: "glow from within." — lands on Frame 5 with full lip-sync; closed-lip serene composure on all other frames.

Audio note: ambient liquid-electronic bed — deep sub-hum, water-drip percussion, shimmering glow-pulse pads and a soft synth swell, kept low under the spoken line.

Identity safety: AIRA, age 25, adult and non-sexualized; modest liquid-sheen wardrobe; transforms ride on glow-liquid, piping and light layers only, never implying bare skin or touching the face/body. Across all six frames her flow is liquid, weightless and hypnotic.

## Frame 2 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium close, chest-up, subject and raised dropper fill about 58% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the slow push continues with a faint 3° tilt up following the glow-drop (one flowing move), otherwise stable.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0.

CHOREOGRAPHY & FLUID MOTION: her tutting hand folds a crisp geometric line then melts it into a liquid curve as the dropper hand floats up — a seamless weightless transfer that flows directly out of Frame 1's wave, finger-tutting reading as liquid geometry, soft elbows and rolling wrists, nothing snapped or held. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: the tutting fingers fold a right-angle, the wrist rolls it to a curve, the dropper floats to the cheekbone, the spine uncurls through centre.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–3.2s — Aira lifts the dropper near her cheekbone and releases a single luminous glow-drop that beads and hangs in the dark air.
- 3.2–6.0s — the first glow-motes catch UV around her as her eyes cool with hypnotic anticipation and her tutting hand melts to a curve. One clean continuous phrase, real weightless physics throughout.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: continuous life under the action — breath moving the chest and piping-glow, the ponytail carrying a soft sway-settle as she tilts up, the temple strand drifting, micro weight-shifts rolling liquid through the supporting leg, living eye saccades and a natural slow blink; no freeze, no robotic hold between beats.

LIGHTING: cool UV beauty key with soft fill; a cyan-magenta rim catching the drop and stray motes into glowing points; the void pure black; glow-pool living below.

ENVIRONMENT: infinity void with the first glow-motes catching UV in the air, glow-pool reflection below.

AUDIO: diegetic only. Ambient — low deep void-hum throughout. SFX — a soft dropper-release tick at 1.0s, a delicate glow-pulse shimmer at 3.2s, a faint liquid-sheen fabric-shift at 5.6s (gentle attack, long soft tail), a single water-drip accent at 4.8s. Reaction — a soft slow breath at 3.4s. The master track lifts a touch — the deep sub joined by a soft water-drip percussion and a shimmering pad rising under the SFX (present, never full). VO — none this frame.

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:03–0:06 of 18s total).

LIP-SYNC: lips actively shaping the spoken line "glow from within." — natural, soft, in time.

ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

# CONCEPT 18 — SMOKE & SILK WIND TUNNEL

Title: Smoke & Silk Wind Tunnel (Pillar 12 — Dramatic Editorial).

Format: High-Fashion Wind-Tunnel Beauty Hero / Smoke-and-Silk Editorial (dramatic warm-charcoal lane — a deliberate warm, brooding counter to Concept 17's cool glow-void; hard editorial side-light through driven silk and smoke) — SILENT REEL (no voice, music + diegetic only).

Hero Product: "Veil" satin-finish setting mist — a tall smoked-glass column bottle with a brushed-graphite collar and a fine atomizer, a soft translucent amber mist that hangs and catches the hard key; accent hero a single suspended mist-plume drifting in the wind.

Setting: a dark editorial wind-tunnel studio — a deep charcoal-to-graphite seamless cyclorama, a single dramatic warm-amber hard side-key raking across, a wind machine driving streams of fine silk and curling smoke through the air, faint floating dust catching the beam, a polished dark floor throwing a soft reflection (dramatic warm-charcoal lane, NOT bright or busy).

Wardrobe: a sculptural floor-length silk-georgette gown in oyster-ivory and champagne, fully modest (high neck, long sleeves, opaque), with vast streaming panels of fabric caught in the wind so the silk billows in dramatic ribbons; describe drape and movement, never body shape.

Hair: long sleek glossy hair with a deep side part, blown back and streaming dramatically in the wind into a clean editorial line, high gloss, a few face-framing strands whipping alive, color locked to reference.

Makeup: sculpted editorial — a soft smoky bronze-taupe eye, a sharp sculpted cheekbone contour, a crisp champagne highlight on the high points, soft-set strong brows, a satin nude-rose lip, dewy real skin; short almond nude-glossy nails, neat cuticles.

Choreography: editorial wind-machine performance — slow powerful model turns, dramatic head-turns and chin lifts, sweeping arm extensions catching the wind, a slow pivot that lets the silk and hair stream, and fabric-catch poses; sharp-yet-fluid editorial movement with constant wind-driven follow-through, commanding and weighted, never stiff, wrists and fingers always alive.

Movement / Transform: silk-to-smoke ribbon morph (AX.5) — on a sweeping turn the trailing edge of her streaming silk panel dissolves into curling ribbons of soft amber smoke that twist and stream through the wind, then settle back into silk; the morph rides the silk edge and air only, never skin or face.

Concept Line: NONE — this is a silent reel; the concept lands purely through the dramatic silk-and-smoke morph and the product reveal.

Duration: 18s | 6 frames (3s each).

Mood: DRAMATIC EDITORIAL.

Composition: 9:16 vertical; the commanding subject centred against deep charcoal, vast negative space for the silk and smoke to stream.

Grade: warm dramatic charcoal — graphite-and-charcoal shadow, warm amber hard-key highlights, oyster-ivory silk, sharp editorial contrast held rich and uncrushed, full mid-tone, bright editorial vibrance, no harsh clipping.

Spoken line: NONE — silent reel. No VO on any frame; closed-lip serene editorial composure throughout.

Audio note: cinematic editorial bed — a deep cinematic drone, a soft wind-swell, a slow heartbeat-pulse and airy strings, carrying the whole reel since there is no voice (present, atmospheric, never a full mix), under diegetic wind and silk SFX.

Identity safety: AIRA, age 25, adult and non-sexualized; modest streaming silk wardrobe; the morph rides the silk edge, smoke and air only, never implying bare skin or touching the face/body. Across all six frames her movement is powerful, wind-driven and brooding.

MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) the free arm extends slow into the wind, fingertips leading the line; (b) the spine carries its tall counter-rotation; (c) the chin lifts as the gaze rolls to lens; (d) the silk panels and hair stream back together, driven and alive.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–3.0s — Aira tilts the smoked-glass column a few degrees into the hard amber key, warm speculars sliding along the glass as her free arm finishes its extension into the wind.
- 3.0–6.0s — she rolls a commanding brooding gaze to lens on a slow inhale as the silk streams its fullest and her fingertip settles. One clean continuous phrase, real wind physics throughout.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: never frozen — a constant low-amplitude life runs under everything: the breath visibly moving the chest and the silk, the streaming hair carrying a continuous wind-driven sway, the temple strand whipping, micro weight-shifts rolling through the planted leg, the vast silk panels rippling and snapping softly, and the eyes making tiny living saccades with a natural slow blink; absolutely no mannequin hold, no statue freeze, no robotic snap.

LIGHTING: warm hard side-key with soft warm shadow-fill; live amber speculars travelling across glass and skin as the column tips; the cyclorama held deep; warm rim steady on the streaming hair and shoulder; faint amber floor-bounce alive.

ENVIRONMENT: dark editorial wind-tunnel studio, charcoal cyclorama, driven silk and faint curling smoke, floating dust in the beam, polished dark floor.

Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-wide, head-to-thigh, subject fills about 65% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a slow arc easing to a gentle settle at the crest of the turn (one flowing move), no whip.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0.

CHOREOGRAPHY & FLUID MOTION: the apex turn — both arms open wide in a powerful curving editorial sweep following the smoke-ribbons as the body spirals to its fullest, the trailing panels streaming vapour; a single expansive wind-tunnel turn, weighted, whirling and continuous, the most flowing beat of the whole phrase. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) both arms sweep wide to frame the ribbons; (b) the spine reaches its fullest spiral; (c) the smoke-ribbons twist and stream; (d) the gaze lifts commanding to lens.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–4.0s — the trailing silk panels burst fully into vast twisting ribbons of amber smoke streaming around her as she completes the editorial turn.
- 4.0–6.0s — she sweeps both arms wide and lifts a brooding commanding gaze to lens as the smoke-ribbons crest. One impossible move at full crest, real silk-to-vapour physics, the smoke streaming only from the silk and air.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: alive at the peak — breath at the top of the inhale moving the chest, strands and silk lifting in the driven air, micro weight-shifts keeping the turn liquid, the smoke-ribbons carrying continuous internal turbulence, living eyes with a slow blink; absolutely no freeze, no statue hold even at the hero beat.

SFX — a full warm smoke-whoosh-swell cresting at 2.0s as the silk bursts (full but not harsh), a flurry of soft vapour-curls 2.0–5.0s, a driven silk-flutter at 4.0s. Reaction — a brooding soft exhale at 4.0s. The master track reaches its peak — cinematic drone, heartbeat-pulse and swelling cello cresting with an airy string lift (present, never full). VO — none (silent reel).

LIP-SYNC: none — silent reel; lips closed and serene.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, double-exposure unless intended, audio desync, wardrobe drift, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (silk-smoke/ribbon/hair/light), smoke touching the face or skin, instant smoke pop-in with no curl, mannequin posture, robotic snapping motion, stiff joints, frozen background, identity flicker.

# CONCEPT 19 — STARLIGHT BALLROOM WALTZ

Title: Starlight Ballroom Waltz (Pillar 13 — Dreamy Celestial).

Format: Celestial Ballroom Beauty Hero / Starlight-Editorial (cool midnight-blue lane — a deliberate cool, dreamy counter to Concept 18's warm-charcoal wind-tunnel; soft moonlight and starfield through open arches) — SPOKEN REEL.

Hero Product: "Astra" illuminating starlight serum — a faceted celestial-blue crystal flacon with a brushed-silver star-cap and a soft dome applicator, a deep-blue liquid scattered with fine suspended light-flecks like a captured galaxy that throws cool diamond speculars; accent hero a single suspended luminous drop catching the starlight.

Setting: a grand abandoned celestial ballroom open to the night sky — soaring dark-marble columns, a cracked grand floor of dark polished marble reflecting starlight, tall arched windows open to a deep midnight-blue starfield, drifting motes of stardust, soft moonlight pooling through the arches, a faint teal-violet aurora at the horizon (cool dreamy celestial lane, NOT warm or busy).

Wardrobe: a sweeping floor-length ballgown in midnight-blue silk-tulle, fully modest (fitted high-neck bodice, long sleeves, opaque), with a vast layered tulle skirt scattered with tiny crystal-star embellishments that catch the moonlight, the skirt blooming wide in the waltz turns; describe drape and movement, never body shape.

Hair: a soft romantic updo with loose face-framing tendrils, a faint silver-stardust sheen, high gloss, a delicate crystal-star pin at the crown, color locked to reference.

Makeup: celestial luminous — a soft silver-and-midnight smoked eye with fine star-glitter at the inner corner, dewy glass skin with a cool diamond highlight on the high points, soft-set strong brows, a satin rose-nude lip with a wet light-point, a scatter of tiny constellation freckle-dots across the cheekbone in soft shimmer, dewy real skin; short almond pearl-glossy nails, neat cuticles.

Choreography: solo-waltz — sweeping rise-and-fall waltz turns, graceful spotting spins, a rise to demi-pointe with a soaring arm-line, gown-blooming pivots and romantic épaulement of the shoulders; the movement floats and rises-and-falls like a waltz, buoyant and continuous, soft joints and long lines, never stiff, the tulle skirt always blooming and alive.

Movement / Transform: gown-to-galaxy starfield bloom (AX.5) — on a sweeping waltz turn the hem of her tulle skirt dissolves upward into a vast swirling galaxy of stars, nebula-mist and constellation-light that spirals around her, then settles back into the crystal-star skirt; the bloom rides the skirt hem and air only, never skin or face.

Concept Line: "dance among the stars."

Duration: 21s | 7 frames (3s each).

Mood: DREAMY CELESTIAL.

Composition: 9:16 vertical; the floating subject centred against the midnight starfield, vast cool negative space above and beside her for the gown-galaxy to swirl.

Grade: cool celestial — midnight-blue and indigo midtones, silver-and-diamond starlight highlights, soft moon-glow, a faint teal-violet aurora kiss, clean uncrushed blacks, full mid-tone, bright luminous vibrance, no harsh clipping.

Spoken line: lands Frame 5 — "dance among the stars." (English, natural Indian accent, soft and wondering.)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic (a soft tulle-whisper, a faint night-air void-tone, a delicate crystal star-chime as the galaxy blooms) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a dreamy celestial 3/4 waltz with soft strings, harp, celesta and glass-bell shimmer, kept low under the spoken line.

Identity safety: AIRA, age 25, adult and non-sexualized; modest tulle ballgown; the bloom rides the skirt hem, stars and air only, never implying bare skin or touching the face/body. Across all seven frames her movement floats and rises like a waltz, weightless and dreamy.

## Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a single slow 8% push-in across the clip with a barely-there float, otherwise stable — one move only, no whip.

MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: the free arm floats up, the spine rises, the leg breathes through the plié, the skirt settles from the last sway.

## Frame 4 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-wide, head-to-thigh, subject fills about 65% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a slow arc easing to a gentle settle at the crest of the turn (one flowing move), no whip.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0.

CHOREOGRAPHY & FLUID MOTION: the apex turn — both arms open wide in a soaring curving balletic sweep following the galaxy as the body spirals to its fullest on demi-pointe, the tulle streaming starlight; a single expansive waltz turn, weightless, whirling and continuous, the most flowing beat of the whole phrase. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) both arms sweep wide to frame the galaxy; (b) the spine reaches its fullest rising spiral; (c) the galaxy wheels and streams around her; (d) the gaze lifts wondering to lens.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–4.0s — the skirt blooms fully into a vast swirling galaxy of stars and nebula-mist wheeling around her as she completes the waltz turn.
- 4.0–6.0s — she sweeps both arms wide and lifts a dreamy wondering gaze to lens as the galaxy crests. One impossible move at full crest, real gown-to-starlight physics, the stars streaming only from the skirt and air.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: alive at the peak — breath at the top of the inhale moving the chest, tendrils and tulle lifting in the swirl, micro weight-shifts keeping the turn liquid, the galaxy carrying continuous internal drift, living eyes with a slow blink; absolutely no freeze, no statue hold even at the hero beat.

LIGHTING: cool moonlight key with soft fill; a brilliant cool silver rim racing live along the streaming galaxy and tulle; the ballroom held deep; the galaxy-glow and aurora kiss flaring strongest.

ENVIRONMENT: celestial ballroom at the transform crest, a vast galaxy of stars wheeling and streaming through the air.

AUDIO: diegetic only. Ambient — faint airy void-tone and starfield shimmer. SFX — a full soft star-bloom-swell cresting at 2.0s as the skirt blooms (full but not harsh), a cascade of delicate star-chimes 2.0–5.0s, a soft tulle-flutter at 4.0s. Reaction — a wondering soft exhale at 4.0s. The master track reaches its peak — harp, celesta, glass-bell and airy strings cresting with a luminous swell (present, never full). VO — none this frame.

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:09–0:12 of 21s total).

HANDS & NAILS: right hand cradling the faceted celestial-blue flacon at heart height with the soft dome applicator poised, matched to Frame 1; left hand extended in a final graceful port-de-bras line already curving back toward the opening waltz frame, fingers soft and alive; short almond nails in a pearl gloss, cuticles neat, no white-knuckle tension.

MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) the afterglow port-de-bras from Frame 6 completes its arc and the arms curve inward and upward toward the opening line; (b) the supporting heel lifts the body back onto a poised demi-pointe as the skirt catches a soft updraft; (c) the gaze travels back to the starlit hook that opened the reel; (d) the whole body floats into the held opening shape, buoyant and mid-breath, so the next frame is Frame 1.

BACKGROUND — the grand celestial ballroom with dark-marble columns, the starlit polished-marble floor and the arched windows onto the midnight starfield, matched to Frame 1.

## Frame 7 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: full-length vertical hero of the waltz resolving to the opening shape, subject fills about 60% of the vertical frame — matched to Frame 1.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a whisper-slow push easing to a gentle settle and drifting back a hair at the very end so the final framing matches Frame 1 exactly for the loop point — one flowing move, almost imperceptible, dreamlike.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0, shallow celestial depth, creamy fall-off into the starfield.

CHOREOGRAPHY & FLUID MOTION: the closing breath of a waltz phrase folding back to its first position — the dance completing its endless circle, decelerating into the exact start pose through pure flow so play-through loops with no seam. MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: (a) 0:18–0:19 the afterglow port-de-bras completes its arc, arms curving inward and lifting toward the opening line; (b) 0:19–0:20 the heel floats the body up onto a poised demi-pointe as the skirt hem catches a soft updraft and the gaze travels back to the opening hook; (c) 0:20–0:21 the whole figure settles into the held opening shape, mid-breath and weightless, landing precisely on the Frame 1 pose. Movement is buoyant, continuous and liquid, a rise-and-fall waltz cadence, never a stop-start or a snap.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0:18–0:19 — her arms arc through the afterglow line and begin curving back toward the opening waltz frame; 0:19–0:20 — she rises to a poised demi-pointe as the crystal-star skirt lifts on a soft updraft; 0:20–0:21 — her gaze returns to the starlit hook and she settles into the held opening shape, breath suspended for the loop. One clean continuous loop-resolve, real weightless physics.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: continuous life all the way into the loop — a fluid spine and arm flow, a soft settling blink, breath rising through the ribcage, tulle hem and hair tendrils drifting on the updraft, stardust motes wheeling slowly — matched to Frame 1 so the cycle never reveals a freeze or a seam; absolutely no mannequin hold, no robotic snap, no joint stiffness, the body floating even in the held final beat.

LIGHTING: identical to Frame 1 — cool moonlight key from frame-left, silver rim off the void, soft marble bounce; starlight glints pulse gently on the crystal-stars and flacon facets as she settles.

ENVIRONMENT: the grand abandoned celestial ballroom returned to its opening state — dark-marble columns, starlit polished-marble floor, arched windows onto the midnight starfield, drifting stardust, a faint teal-violet aurora at the horizon.

AUDIO: diegetic only. Ambient — a faint airy void-tone matched to Frame 1. SFX — a soft diegetic tulle-whisper at 0:19 as the skirt lifts, a last gentle star-chime at 0:20 as she settles. Reaction — a soft breath at 0:19. The master track resolves on a gentle suspended waltz-chord (soft strings, harp and a final glass-bell shimmer) that loops seamlessly back into the Frame 1 figure with no audible seam (present, never full). VO — none (the line landed Frame 5).

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:18–0:21 of 21s total), completing the seven-frame reel.

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps acquisition, 180° shutter, natural cinematic motion blur on the drifting tulle, hair and stardust; playback real-time, loop-matched to Frame 1, no strobing.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips return to the composed serene half-smile of the Frame 1 set; no dialogue on this frame.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, double-exposure unless intended, audio desync, wardrobe drift, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (skirt-reform/star/stardust/hair/light), galaxy failing to resolve back to the crystal-star skirt, stars touching the face or skin, mannequin posture, robotic snapping motion, stiff joints, frozen background, identity flicker, visible loop seam.

MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Concept 19 — full reel, 21s, anchored at the Frame 3–4 gown-to-galaxy starfield bloom and the Frame 5 line "dance among the stars."): a dreamy celestial 3/4 waltz in a cool midnight key — soft sustained strings, a single rolling harp figure, a glassy celesta and a glass-bell shimmer over a barely-there sub-pulse, with a fine star-chime tracing the drifting stardust. Tempo gentle and floating around 84–90 BPM, key cool major-with-a-wistful-lift, weightless and luminous throughout. The bed opens hushed under the opening waltz-stance (Frames 1–2), the celesta and glass-bells rising as the skirt begins to dissolve into stars (Frame 3), swelling to its fullest weightless bloom — strings, harp arpeggio and a wheeling wash of glass-bell shimmer like a turning galaxy — across the transform peak (Frame 4), then easing back so the spoken line "dance among the stars." sits clear and intimate over a hushed string pad (Frame 5), drifting through the afterglow port-de-bras passage (Frame 6) and resolving on a gentle suspended waltz-chord with a last soft star-chime as the waltz folds back to its opening for the loop (Frame 7) with no audible seam. Keep it woven under the diegetic tulle-whisper, airy void-tone and pinpoint star-chime SFX — dreamy and present, never a full driving mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

# CONCEPT 20 — PHOENIX COUTURE FINALE

Title: Pillar 14 — Triumphant Finale (rebirth-couture fire beauty)

Format: Phoenix Couture Beauty Hero / Triumphant Fire-Editorial (BF category — radiant warm rebirth glow) — SPOKEN REEL, the grand finale of the set.

Hero Product: "Ascendant" radiant rebirth elixir — a flame-shaped amber-glass flacon with a sculpted rose-gold feather-cap and a soft dome applicator, a warm molten-gold elixir threaded with fine suspended gold flecks like banked embers; accent hero a single suspended ember-drop catching the dawn light.

Setting: a vast obsidian-and-gold cathedral hall at first light — towering arched windows pouring warm sunrise gold across a polished black-marble floor that mirrors the glow, soaring dark columns veined with gold, drifting ember-motes and fine ash-light suspended in the warm air, a single shaft of dawn pooling around her (warm triumphant-fiery lane — deliberate warm counter to Concept 19's cool celestial midnight).

Wardrobe: a dramatic floor-length couture gown in deep ember-red bleeding into molten-gold, a structured feather-textured bodice with a modest high neckline and long fitted sleeves, and a vast sweeping cape-train that arcs through the air on the rise; fully modest, opaque, sculptural, the silk-and-organza drinking the dawn into glowing folds.

Hair: a sleek sculptural high crown-updo with a razor-clean part and a soft rose-gold sheen, a few precise face-framing strands, a fine ash-gold shimmer catching the warm rim, color locked to reference.

Makeup: triumphant fiery: a warm gold-to-ember gradient lid with a blazing rose-gold inner-corner, dewy radiant real skin, a sharp gold highlight on the high points, soft-set brows, a bold molten coral-red lip with a wet light-point, real pores and fine vellus texture preserved; short almond nails in a warm rose-gold gloss.

Choreography: powerful contemporary — grounded floor-rooted contracions opening into a soaring full-body spiral rise, sweeping cape-arcs carved by the arms, a powerful releve to full extension, and broad triumphant port-de-bras; every movement is weighted, fluid and continuous, the body uncoiling like a flame finding its height, soft joints and long lines throughout, never a held or rigid pose.

Movement / Transform: ember-gather → ember-to-feather phoenix wing rise (the one impossible move) — the embers banked along the sweeping cape-train gather and bloom upward into vast luminous phoenix wings of golden feather-light that spread and wheel behind her on the rise, then settle into a soft radiant feather-mantle of glints. Embers and wings ride the cape and the air only, never the skin or face.

Concept Line: "rise."

Duration: 24 seconds | 8 frames

Mood: TRIUMPHANT FIERY

Composition: centered triumphant hero with tall warm negative space above and beside her for the cape-arc and the phoenix wings to spread.

Grade: warm triumphant-fiery — molten-gold and ember-red midtones, rose-gold and white-gold highlights, a warm dawn rim, rich uncrushed blacks, full mid-tone, bright radiant vibrance, no harsh clipping (warm rebirth lane).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — "rise." (English, natural Indian accent, low, certain and triumphant.)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic (a warm cathedral room tone, a soft ember-crackle, a feather-whoosh, a low resonant hall-swell) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a triumphant orchestral-cinematic bed with soaring strings, a brass swell, soft choir and a low taiko/timpani pulse, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult; the gown is fully modest; the mood is triumphant, powerful and radiant, never sexualized; the embers and wings ride the cape and air, never the skin; movement is choreographic and grand.

Across all eight frames her movement uncoils and soars like a flame finding its height, powerful, fluid and triumphant.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium close, chest-up, subject and raised applicator fill about 58% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the slow push continues with a faint 3° tilt up following the rising applicator (one flowing move), otherwise stable.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0.

MOVEMENT BREAKDOWN: the root leg presses to lift, the spine uncoils taller, the applicator arm rises along the cheekbone, the cape-train lifts off the marble.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0:03–0:04.6 — Aira sweeps the Ascendant applicator along her high cheekbone to a radiant warm glow and releases a single gold-flecked drop; 0:04.6–0:06 — the first embers wake along the cape-edge as her eyes kindle with quiet certainty and her free arm completes its rising arc. One clean continuous phrase, real physics throughout.

MICRO-MOVEMENT / ANTI-STIFFNESS: continuous life under the action — breath moving the chest and cape, the crown-updo carrying a soft settle as she tilts up, the temple strand drifting, micro weight-shifts rolling off the grounded leg, living eye saccades and a natural slow blink; no freeze, no robotic hold between beats.

## Frame 8 of 8 — VIDEO PROMPT

ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Concept 20 — full reel, one continuous 24-second triumphant orchestral-cinematic bed, anchored at the Frame 4 phoenix-wing apex and the Frame 6 spoken line): compose a single unbroken score for the whole reel rather than per-shot stings. Open (0:00–0:06) with a lone warm sustained string kindling under the cathedral room tone, joined by a soft low choir pad as the first embers wake — quiet, banked, expectant. Build (0:06–0:09) with rising strings and a first soft brass kindling as the spiral rise lifts and the embers climb. Crest (0:09–0:12) into the warm apex — soaring strings, a full brass swell, open choir and a soft taiko/timpani pulse blooming with the full phoenix-wing spread, lifted yet held under the diegetic feather-whoosh. Settle (0:12–0:15) easing strings and choir down, brass receding, the taiko fading to a low warm pulse as the wings resolve to the mantle. Underscore the spoken beat (0:15–0:18) with a sustained warm string-and-choir glow and a soft brass halo ducked beneath the single word “rise.”, never competing with the voice. Flourish (0:18–0:21) with a soft warm reprise glow — strings, a gentle brass halo and light choir — a warm victory-lap shimmer under the cape-sweep. Resolve (0:21–0:24) back to a soft warm sustained string glow that matches the opening kindling for a seamless loop. Keep everything warm, soaring and ember-lit, ducked under the diegetic ember-crackle, feather-whoosh and cape-whoosh and fully under the spoken line; the bed should feel triumphant and present, never a full driving mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)
